reasoning: To be approved by December 31, 2025, an ETF would typically need an active, well-publicized 19b-4 application that the SEC has been reviewing for months (up to the 240-day limit), along with an S-1 form. There are no credible reports of any asset manager having filed for a spot Pepe ETF, let alone possessing a pending application nearing an approval deadline. The SEC has historically required underlying regulated futures markets (like CME) of significant size for crypto ETFs (like BTC and ETH), which PEPE entirely lacks. Given the fact that we are less than a month away from the deadline and no filing currently exists, it is functionally impossible for the SEC to file and approve an ETF in this timeframe. The Polymarket price on November 27 stood at 2%, correctly reflecting an almost non-existent chance. I'm choosing 0.02, representing the lowest possible estimate within the typical platform range.
